advantages: Per-project isolation prevents version conflicts and system pollution.
venv is built in — no extra install needed.
Environments are disposable: delete the folder to reset.
Makes setups reproducible for teammates and CI.
disadvantages: Must remember to activate; forgetting installs into the wrong place.
venv only manages Python packages, not system libraries (e.g. CUDA, ffmpeg).
Activation differs across shells/OSes, tripping up beginners.
Each env duplicates packages on disk.
